title: A Hymn To Bacchus
content:
Bacchus, let me drink no more!
Wild are seas that want a shore!
When our drinking has no stint,
There is no one pleasure in't.
I have drank up for to please
Thee, that great cup, Hercules.
Urge no more; and there shall be
Daffadils giv'n up to thee.
meaning:
Robert Herrick's contribution to classic is further solidified by the brilliance found in "A Hymn To Bacchus"...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
